data.title: Altimmune set to release earnings on 2026-03-05 with expected EPS of $-0.25. Previous quarter EPS beat by $0.07, resulting in 4.31% stock increase..setPositiveButton management guidance could impact stock price more than earnings beat/miss. Shares trading at $4.16 as of March 03.
data.description: Altimmune (NASDAQ: ALT) is set to announce its quarterly earnings on March 5, 2026. Analysts predict an earnings per share (EPS) of -$0.25. Investors are eagerly anticipating if Altimmune will surpass this forecast and offer positive guidance for the next quarter which can heavily impact the stock price. In the previous quarter, Altimmune exceeded expectations by $0.07, resulting in a 4.31% increase in share price the following day. Here is a summary of Altimmune's recent earnings performance: - Q3 2025: EPS Estimate -$0.28, EPS Actual -$0.21, Price Change 4.31%- Q2 2025: EPS Estimate -$0.32, EPS Actual -$0.27, Price Change 3.31%- Q1 2025: EPS Estimate -$0.35, EPS Actual -$0.26, Price Change -1.04%- Q4 2024: EPS Estimate -$0.34, EPS Actual -$0.33, Price Change 3.58%. Altimmune's shares were trading at $4.16 as of March 3. This data highlights the importance of both actual earnings and future guidance in influencing the stock's performance.

data.title: Ondas Holdings (ONDS) stock up on $20 million order for autonomous border protection system. Airobotics Ltd. received the purchase order under a strategic national government tender, marking a crucial milestone as prime contractor in multi-year framework.
data.description: Ondas Holdings' shares rose following a $20 million purchase order for an autonomous border protection system, with its subsidiary, Airobotics Ltd., securing the order under a government tender. The order is part of a multi-year framework, with further development milestones and deployment phases expected. The order follows Ondas' appointment as prime contractor for a national security program in December 2025. The initiative aims to implement an AI-driven security network using drones and advanced software to protect key areas. The broader market, with the Nasdaq up 0.73%, contributed to the stock's positive movement, reflecting a favorable climate for technology stocks.

data.title: Good Times USA transitions to employee-owned company through ESOP, making employees direct stakeholders in its success.
data.description: In Tampa, Florida, Good Times USA has recently shifted to an Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP), which is a retirement benefit involving company stock. This move means that employees will gradually receive company shares at no expense to them as the company prospers, leading to an increase in share value. Upon retirement, employees can sell their shares back to the company. Consequently, the company will eventually be entirely owned by its employees, allowing them to have a direct stake in its ongoing success.

data.title: HooRii launching Kickstarter campaign on March 15 for ClawStage, aiming for $1 million in crowdfunding for AI device.
data.description: In Hong Kong, HooRii revealed their plan to kick off a Kickstarter campaign for the ClawStage on March 15. The innovative product aims to cater to creators, educators, developers, and tech enthusiasts as a fully integrated AI device. The campaign has set a target of reaching $1 million in crowdfunding support. ClawStage signifies HooRii's progression towards integrating AI into tangible applications.

data.description: In the bustling city of New York, on March 4, 2026, Yarbo, a manufacturer specializing in yard robotics, made waves with the release of their cutting-edge M Series. The company's latest innovation managed to secure an impressive over US$1 million in funding in just 2 hours through the popular crowdfunding platform, Kickstarter. This achievement was made possible thanks to the enthusiastic support of more than 200 backers, indicating a significant level of interest in the market prior to its official retail launch. data.title: Daktronics, Inc. (DAKT) shares dropped after Q3 FY26 results: Adjusted EPS at 9 cents (vs 13-cent estimate), sales of $181.87 million (vs $181 million estimate); Sales rose 21.6% Y/Y, adjusted operating income increased to $4 million.
data.description: Shares of Daktronics, Inc. (NASDAQ: DAKT) dipped after the company released its third-quarter FY26 results. The report showed mixed performance, with an adjusted EPS of nine cents falling short of the estimated 13 cents, while sales reached $181.87 million, surpassing the forecasted $181 million. Sales saw a 21.6% increase year over year, attributed to efficient backlog fulfillment at the start of the quarter. Adjusted operating income also saw a significant rise to $4 million from $1.2 million compared to the same period last year. The company anticipates this trend to continue into the final quarter of fiscal 2026. These details and more are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Wall Street rises on Iran diplomacy hopes. Nasdaq up 1.5% to 25,100, S&P 500 up 0.8% to 6,875. Dow underperforms, down 0.1% to 48,860. Bitcoin surges to $71,000, up 7%. Energy sector lags, XLE down 1.2%. Technology leads, XLK up 1.8%.
data.description: Wall Street experienced a relief rally with reports of potential U.S.-Iran diplomatic talks, mainly driven by a New York Times article hinting at diplomatic dialogue. By midday trading in New York, the Nasdaq 100 gained 1.5% to 25,100, the S&P 500 rose by 0.8% to 6,875, and the Russell 2000 added 1.0% to 2,630. However, the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 0.1% to 48,860, influenced by consumer staples weakness. The CBOE Volatility Index dropped 11.6% to 20.84. Technology sectors led the gains, with the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund up 1.8%. Other sectors like consumer discretionary and communications also saw gains. Energy was the session's laggard with the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund down 1.2%. Bitcoin surged, reaching $71,000 for the first time in a month, rising by 7% and boosting other cryptocurrencies following the trend of de-escalation. Oil prices fell after two consecutive sessions of rallying, and the technology sector advanced as ceasefire odds rose in prediction markets. (character count: 980)
